body.u: Over the past fifty years:- when I was sixteen years of age. I had just left school on the 16th May 1978. I signed on the dole and the 17th May because I was not going to start work until the 31st May. My first job was in a fuse factory I never thought that I would see the day when I would work in a factory. I had to catch the 8 minutes past seven train to be at work for 8 o clock. Then I had to work my hardest until Quarter past five. That was Mondays to Thursdays. I used to look forward to Fridays in those days, because I got a good wage, and we left work at 1.00pm.

On the 5th August 1978 I left home and I went to live with my nan in Northamptonshire. It was a big step to take at the time, but itsit's what I wanted to do. I had a job there waiting for me in a shoe factory, there I became a sprayer I worked with an old friend whose name was Karen. We used to have a good laugh in the spraying room, and we got on ever so well together. I worked there for four years until I was almost 21. Then I did a good days work. I got married without any doubts. I married an old boyfriend who I had liked from my sweet old age 16. His name is Mick and he is still very good to me. He has never said a word out of place, I moved to his hometown which is in Corby. And we were very happy and we still are now. We used to go on holidays a lot when we were first married, we went to Spain and Italy and travelled most of the world. That went on for two years. Then I found out I was having a baby when I was 23, Mick and I were both very pleased about this, but we knew we would not be able to go on holidays and go out at night as much as we used too.

Well I had my baby boy and I called it after his father Michael. He was a very nice baby and I had no trouble with him at all. Mick and I took him to Paris with us when he was only 9 months old. We didn’t think it would be a relaxing holiday not with the baby along with us, but we had a lovely time and we enjoyed ourselfsourselves very much.

When Michael was two years old I found out I was having another baby. So I had to stop in and rest a lot. While Mick used to go out and enjoy himself with his mates. But it didn’t bother me as much as it did when I was having Michael. Anyway 9 months later I had a baby girl. I named it Sylvana after my mum. She was a lovely baby also. She only weighed 5lb when I had her I was a bit worried at first but she was alright.

Eight years later my two children were both at school and enjoying it twotoo. So I thought I should go out and work again. So I went out looking for a job and I managed to get one in Marks and Spencers as a shop Assistant. It was a big change from working in a factory. And I was not as young now I was almost 34 years old. But I’m glad to say I didn’t feel it at all.

My husband Mick had an accident in his car. I was at work and the phone rang to tell me to go home quickly. So I went and Mick was already taken into hospital. He had crashed into the back of a lorry coming back from London where he had just been to his Aunty’s funeral. I phoned him in Northampton Hospital. I had my car then so I went all the way over to Northampton. Very upset of what I might see when I got there. But things were not so bad. He had a broken leg and head injuries. So he stayed in hospital for two months. He came out of hospital on my 35th birthday. We got a babysitter in and we went out celebrating. It was good to have him home again.

Ten years had gone by and my children had grown up so fast. Michael was now 22 years old and engaged to a lovely girl who is now his wife and I am a Grandmother at the age of 45 years of age. My daughter Sylvana has just got engaged and she will be married in two years time. She is now 20 years of age. My husband Mick bought a new car. It’s what he always wanted a Ford Mustang. It was a lovely car. We used to go on holiday a lot with my son and his wife and my daughter and her boyfriend Tommy. As the years went by I had lost my mum and dad, my nans and granddads. It was very rare I saw my two brother because they lived abroad in Italy with theretheir Italian wives and children.

Mick and I had both turned 60 years of age. And we look back now thinking of the happy years we had had together, not knowing how much longer we are going to live. Our daughter and son were both married and we were grandparents to eight children. We used to spoil all of them. And they used to stay at our big bungalow at the weekends they never missed. While there mums and dads went out enjoying thereselfsthereselves like Mick and I used to do. Yes we’ve had some good memories to look back on and both of our children have been very good right from the time they were baby’sbabies. We’ve led a good life together and I hope my children and their children lead good life also as Mick and I have done.
Mrs Kay Bullimoore writing from the past of her good memorial life.
